perturbation.original_text: 2. Scope of the Joint Venture Business. The Joint Venture is formed for the purpose of engaging generally in the business of marketing batteries and related products, (the "Products") that include the display of licensed logos, images, brand names and other labels that differentiate them from the branding (the "PVSS Products") under which PVSS and/or its affiliates, sell to retailers and distributors in the normal course of their business. Without in any way limiting the generality of the foregoing, the business of the Joint Venture shall include: (a) The purchase of Products for resale; (b) The acquisition of a license(s) permitting the use of selected images in the Products; (c) The sale and distribution of the Products to retailers and distributors; and, (d) The transaction of such other and further business as is necessary, advisable, or incidental to the business of the Joint Venture. (e) Develop a global marketing program for licensed Products Exhibit A attached hereto, describes by way of example but not limitation the responsibilities of the Joint Venturers
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Scope of the Joint Venture Business. The Joint Venture is formed for the purpose of engaging generally in the business of marketing batteries and related products, (the "Products") that include the display of licensed logos, images, brand names and other labels that differentiate them from the branding (the "PVSS Products") under which PVSS and/or its affiliates, sell to retailers and distributors in the normal course of their business. Without in any way limiting the generality of the foregoing, the business of the Joint Venture shall primarily include the sale and distribution of the Products only to retailers that are part of the PVSS network; all other potential markets are explicitly excluded. Without in any way limiting the generality of the foregoing, the business of the Joint Venture shall include: (a) The purchase of Products for resale; (b) The acquisition of a license(s) permitting the use of selected images in the Products; (c) The sale and distribution of the Products to retailers and distributors; and, (d) The transaction of such other and further business as is necessary, advisable, or incidental to the business of the Joint Venture. (e) Develop a global marketing program for licensed Products Exhibit A attached hereto, describes by way of example but not limitation the responsibilities of the Joint Venturers
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the scope of the Joint Venture's business. The initial part of the modified Section 2 restricts the sales to the PVSS network, while the subsequent clauses (a-e) maintain a broader scope, including sales and distribution to retailers and distributors generally, contradicting the prior restriction. Specifically, this contradicts clause (c) which states the 'sale and distribution of the Products to retailers and distributors;' making it unclear if the Joint Venture is limited to selling only to PVSS retailers or to other retailers and distributors as well.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Scope of the Joint Venture Business. The Joint Venture is formed for the purpose of engaging generally in the business of marketing batteries and related products, (the "Products") that include the display of licensed logos, images, brand names and other labels that differentiate them from the branding (the "PVSS Products") under which PVSS and/or its affiliates, sell to retailers and distributors in the normal course of their business. Without in any way limiting the generality of the foregoing, the business of the Joint Venture shall primarily include the sale and distribution of the Products only to retailers that are part of the PVSS network; all other potential markets are explicitly excluded. Without in any way limiting the generality of the foregoing, the business of the Joint Venture shall include: (a) The purchase of Products for resale; (b) The acquisition of a license(s) permitting the use of selected images in the Products; (c) The sale and distribution of the Products to retailers and distributors; and, (d) The transaction of such other and further business as is necessary, advisable, or incidental to the business of the Joint Venture. (e) Develop a global marketing program for licensed Products Exhibit A attached hereto, describes by way of example but not limitation the responsibilities of the Joint Venturers
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 2 initially restricts sales to the PVSS network, stating "the business of the Joint Venture shall primarily include the sale and distribution of the Products only to retailers that are part of the PVSS network; all other potential markets are explicitly excluded." However, it subsequently states, "Without in any way limiting the generality of the foregoing, the business of the Joint Venture shall include: ... (c) The sale and distribution of the Products to retailers and distributors," directly contradicting the initial restriction. This creates an ambiguity regarding whether the Joint Venture's sales are limited to the PVSS network or extend to other retailers and distributors as well, causing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Capital Contributions. Except as agreed upon by mutual consent, the Joint Venturers shall not be required to make any capital contribution to the Joint Venture.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Capital Contributions. Except as agreed upon by mutual consent, the Joint Venturers shall initially not be required to make any capital contribution to the Joint Venture. However, should the Joint Venture require additional funding after six months, each Joint Venturer will be required to contribute up to $50,000 in capital, proportional to their ownership stake.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction within Section 3. The initial sentence states that no capital contribution is required, while the subsequent sentence mandates a potential capital contribution of up to $50,000 after six months. The contradiction lies in the conflicting requirements for capital contributions within the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Capital Contributions. Except as agreed upon by mutual consent, the Joint Venturers shall initially not be required to make any capital contribution to the Joint Venture. However, should the Joint Venture require additional funding after six months, each Joint Venturer will be required to contribute up to $50,000 in capital, proportional to their ownership stake.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a requirement for capital contributions after six months, directly contradicting the initial statement that 'the Joint Venturers shall initially not be required to make any capital contribution'. This creates a direct conflict within Section 3 itself, making it an in-text contradiction according to the provided definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Term of Joint Venture. The Joint Venture shall commence on the 1st of March, 2003, and shall be effective until February 28, 2004 unless extended by written agreement of the Joint Venturers not less than thirty (30) days prior to scheduled termination.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Term of Joint Venture. The Joint Venture shall commence on the 1st of March, 2003, and shall be effective until February 28, 2004 unless extended by written agreement of either Joint Venturer, provided notification is given not less than thirty (30) days prior to scheduled termination. The joint venture shall be terminated effective June 30, 2003.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a contradiction in Section 8. The first sentence states the joint venture is effective until February 28, 2004, unless extended, implying a potential long-term relationship. However, the added final sentence states that the joint venture shall be terminated effective June 30, 2003, which directly contradicts the previous statement by setting a firm, earlier termination date. The phrase 'either Joint Venturer' also makes the extension requirement ambiguous since that phrase is not what was agreed to (mutual written agreement).
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Term of Joint Venture. The Joint Venture shall commence on the 1st of March, 2003, and shall be effective until February 28, 2004 unless extended by written agreement of either Joint Venturer, provided notification is given not less than thirty (30) days prior to scheduled termination. The joint venture shall be terminated effective June 30, 2003.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 8 introduces a direct contradiction. The original text allowed for the joint venture to continue until February 28, 2004, with a provision for extension. The addition of 'The joint venture shall be terminated effective June 30, 2003' creates a clear and irreconcilable conflict with the original end date, making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable regarding the term of the joint venture. The language 'either Joint Venturer' also makes the extension requirement ambiguous since that phrase is not what was agreed to (mutual written agreement).
